All right, so one concept I really felt was necessary to touch on because it ties in with everything else that we've talked about. So it ties in with giving feedback and it ties in with growing your business and that is that of tolerance. And so the more we tolerate of ourselves, others and the business, then the less likely we are to get feedback, which means the less likely people are to grow, the less likely we are to grow, the less likely the business is to grow. And so oftentimes, and for most of you that are watching this, how you've come to where you are today, went something like this, right? Your life sucked, blah, blah, blah. You didn't want to give up. You couldn't do what you were doing anymore, blah, blah, blah. Somehow out of the depths of hell, you forged this business and then you found your success, right? So it's most success stories go something like that, right? There was a lot of pain and suffering, okay? And so our whole lives were taught then in order to advance, be successful and create something great that we have to suffer, right? Therefore we build an extremely high tolerance to suffering and I developed this tolerance as well. So I wanted to share my story so that you understand where I'm coming from and then I'm not just full of shit and I have not always been this way. So when I was 10, my parents separated and after that my mother indulged alcohol and drugs. She became an addict and I was a kid. I relied on her. I didn't want to live with my dad because I hadn't really known him growing up. He was not home as much. And so I did everything possible, survived by suffering, right? I built up an extremely high tolerance to the chaos that lived in that household. I tolerated being left alone for weeks at a time. I tolerated being physically and verbally abused. I tolerated living a house full of rotten food and actual crap. And I tolerated having strangers in the house at all hours of the time, right? And so I tolerated living in these circumstances and the same tolerance I built as a kid served me in building my career in gym lunch, right? While building gym lunch, I tolerated a lot of stuff. Working 15 hours a day with little to no breaks, sleeping on an extended stay out of our car and not having a real home, eating food from 7-eleven in the gas stations as well as Panda Express, sleeping about five hours a night and literally feeling hungover every day, even though I hadn't drank. The constant stress of what would happen if it didn't work and the constant and brutal failure during those one and a half years, right? That is everything that I tolerated to get the business started. And I tell you this to give you context and understanding as to what I have learned and what I'm still learning, right? Tolerance can help you start or change your career and or business, but it will not help you grow it, okay? This is where many careers end and their business has stopped growing. Is that what got you here will not get you there. Your level of incredible tolerance to suffering is not going to grow the business, okay? At least not a business you want, nor many people would. And when we first started the business, I tolerated an immense amount more than I do right now. You know, I am tolerated. I tolerate people who complained and whined, projects and presentations that were just good enough, immense disorganization, lack of support and structure to processes and lack of time, right? Tolerate a lot. I tolerate people who then weren't doing their jobs well. I tolerated clients who were rude to our staff. I tolerated a lot of things. And why am I telling you all this? Because many of you have grown your businesses this way. And if you're watching this, you're probably really at a crossroads where in order to grow, you must begin to tolerate less, okay? And so why? Why is it that you should tolerate less? Because you get what you tolerate. Your business is the result of your tolerance. And so if you look at people who have the best businesses, they simply tolerate nothing less than that, okay? They demand excellence. They demand things to be the way they are. And if people are not meeting those expectations, they're not on the team, right? What you tolerate becomes your culture, okay? Therefore, the performance of your team is dictated by your level of tolerance to their performance or lack thereof, okay? So I'll give you some examples within our business. This is the way I look at it. Our level of excellence in customer service is dictated by Lily's level of tolerance with her team's performance. Our level of discipline and tenacity in sales is dictated by Dylan's level with his team's performance. And our level of excellence and wow delivered at our annual summits is dictated by Amanda's tolerance with our event planning process, okay? It is ultimately up to them and their level of tolerance. So with that being said, what do you not like about your team? What do you not like about your day? About your work? About your department? What is it right now that gives you the heebie-jeebies? What are you tolerating that you should not be? What is it that you're letting fall by the wayside? What is it? What are the things that keep you up at night that you're not addressing because you've tolerated them for so long, you know nothing else. You just are used to tolerating it, right? And as positive as we may be, identifying the things that make you uncomfortable is how you grow your business, right? And these are pain points or signals that are telling you these are places of opportunity for change and growth, okay? If there are things in your business that are bothering you, it's just an opportunity to change and grow it. You should seek out the pain, investigate the pain. That is how you grow. You don't grow by adding more things, you grow by relief, okay? And so what does this mean? It may mean that you need to block off more time for yourself. Hire or create a new position, restructure your team, let someone go, create new or more training, create a new process or have a hard conversation. Probably a lot of hard conversations, right? And what happens if you don't do this? If you don't embody this concept and believe it to be true? Your tolerance only grows over time. And so what will happen is that over time, as time goes on, your tolerance will continue to raise. If you don't grab it and bring it back down and bring it to life, what happens is you have a business full of chaos. You have a business where you do not like how things are run, where you are stressed, where you don't get the results you want. And what I want you to understand is you have ownership over all of the results in your business because of what you are tolerating.
